subject: Pathology
title: Tumor Markers to Cancer
rows: Choriocarcinoma, Germ cell tumor, Hydatidiform mole

title: Important Criteria & Scores
rows: Cirrhosis prognosis (Bili, Albumin, INR, Ascites, Encephalopathy)
Burns fluid resuscitation (4 mL x kg x %TBSA)

title: Hepatitis B Serology Interpretation
rows: Chronic HBV, low infectivity / inactive carrier
Resolved infection OR occult HBV (isolated core)

subject: Biochemistry
title: Vitamin Deficiency to Disease
rows: Night blindness, Xerophthalmia, Bitot spots, Keratomalacia
Scurvy (bleeding gums, corkscrew hair, perifollicular hemorrhage)

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
title: Vaccine Schedule (India - National Immunization Schedule)
rows: Td-1 (early), Td-2 (4 wks later), Td-Booster if <3 yrs

subject: Dermatology
title: Immunofluorescence (IF) Pattern in Skin Diseases
rows: Linear IgG + C3 along basement membrane (DEJ)
Granular IgA at dermal papillae tips

subject: Pathology
title: Stains to Use
rows: Malaria, Leishmania, H. pylori, blood films

subject: Pathology
title: Pathology Buzzwords - Must-Know One-Liners
points: Owl-eye inclusions = CMV
Reed-Sternberg cell (CD15+, CD30+) = Hodgkin lymphoma
Auer rods = Acute myeloid leukemia (esp. APML, M3)
Smudge cells = Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL)
Tear-drop cells (dacrocytes) = Myelofibrosis
Rouleaux formation = Multiple myeloma
Heinz bodies / Bite cells = G6PD deficiency
Howell-Jolly bodies = Asplenia / post-splenectomy
Psammoma bodies = Papillary thyroid Ca, Serous ovarian Ca, Meningioma, Mesothelioma
Orphan Annie eye nuclei = Papillary thyroid carcinoma
Signet ring cells = Gastric (diffuse) / Krukenberg tumor
Apple-green birefringence (Congo red) = Amyloidosis
Anitschkow cells / Aschoff bodies = Rheumatic heart disease
Negri bodies = Rabies
Councilman bodies = Viral hepatitis / Yellow fever
Mallory bodies (Mallory-Denk) = Alcoholic hepatitis
Lewy bodies = Parkinson disease / DLB
Call-Exner bodies = Granulosa cell tumor
Schiller-Duval bodies = Yolk sac tumor
Verocay bodies = Schwannoma
Russell bodies = Plasma cells (myeloma)
Birbeck granules = Langerhans cell histiocytosis

subject: Pharmacology
title: Drug Side Effects - Must-Know One-Liners
points: Cyclophosphamide = Hemorrhagic cystitis (prevent with MESNA)
Bleomycin / Busulfan / Amiodarone / Methotrexate / Nitrofurantoin = Pulmonary fibrosis
Doxorubicin (Anthracyclines) = Dilated cardiomyopathy
Vincristine = Peripheral neuropathy; Vinblastine = Bone marrow suppression
Cisplatin = Nephrotoxicity + Ototoxicity
Isoniazid = Peripheral neuropathy (give B6), Hepatotoxicity, Drug-induced lupus
Rifampicin = Orange-red secretions, Hepatotoxicity, enzyme inducer
Ethambutol = Optic neuritis (red-green color blindness)
Streptomycin / Aminoglycosides = Ototoxicity + Nephrotoxicity
Pyrazinamide = Hyperuricemia (gout), Hepatotoxicity
ACE inhibitors = Dry cough (bradykinin), Angioedema, Hyperkalemia
Amiodarone = Thyroid dysfunction, Corneal deposits, Blue-grey skin, Pulmonary fibrosis
Statins = Myopathy, Rhabdomyolysis, raised LFTs
Phenytoin = Gingival hyperplasia, Hirsutism, Megaloblastic anemia, Cerebellar ataxia
Valproate = Hepatotoxicity, NTD (teratogen), Weight gain, Tremor
Carbamazepine = SIADH, Aplastic anemia, SJS (HLA-B*1502)
Chloroquine / Hydroxychloroquine = Retinopathy
Vancomycin = Red man syndrome (infusion rate), Nephrotoxicity
Methotrexate = Myelosuppression, Hepatotoxicity, Mucositis (rescue with folinic acid)
Thiazides = Hyperglycemia, Hyperuricemia, Hypercalcemia, Hyperlipidemia, Hypokalemia, Hyponatremia
Clozapine = Agranulocytosis (monitor counts)
Lithium = Nephrogenic DI, Hypothyroidism, Ebstein anomaly (teratogen)

subject: Obstetrics & Gynaecology
title: OBG Management Ladders - Must-Know One-Liners
points: PPH management: Uterine massage > Oxytocin > Ergometrine > Carboprost (PGF2a) > Misoprostol > Bimanual compression > B-Lynch suture > Uterine artery ligation > Internal iliac ligation > Hysterectomy
Eclampsia: MgSO4 (loading + maintenance), control BP (Labetalol/Hydralazine), deliver after stabilization
Shoulder dystocia: HELPERR - Help, Episiotomy, Legs (McRoberts), Pressure (suprapubic), Enter (rotational), Remove posterior arm, Roll over
Magnesium toxicity: stop MgSO4, give Calcium gluconate (first lost = patellar reflex)
PCOS first-line for ovulation induction: Letrozole (preferred over Clomiphene)
Anti-D prophylaxis: Rh-negative mother at 28 weeks + within 72 hrs of delivery of Rh+ baby
Preterm labor <34 weeks: Tocolysis (Nifedipine) + Antenatal corticosteroids + MgSO4 neuroprotection
GDM: Diet/exercise first > Insulin is drug of choice (Metformin alternative)
Cord prolapse: knee-chest position, manual elevation of presenting part, emergency C-section
Active management of third stage: Oxytocin + controlled cord traction + uterine massage

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
title: Biostatistics Formulas - Must-Know One-Liners
points: Sensitivity = TP / (TP + FN) - ability to detect disease (rule OUT if negative, SnNOUT)
Specificity = TN / (TN + FP) - ability to identify healthy (rule IN if positive, SpPIN)
Positive predictive value (PPV) = TP / (TP + FP)
Negative predictive value (NPV) = TN / (TN + FN)
PPV increases with higher prevalence; sensitivity & specificity are prevalence-independent
Positive likelihood ratio = Sensitivity / (1 - Specificity)
Negative likelihood ratio = (1 - Sensitivity) / Specificity
Relative risk (RR) = incidence in exposed / incidence in unexposed (cohort)
Odds ratio (OR) = (a x d) / (b x c) (case-control)
Attributable risk = incidence exposed - incidence unexposed
Number needed to treat (NNT) = 1 / Absolute risk reduction
Incidence = new cases / population at risk; Prevalence = total cases / total population
Standard error of mean = SD / sqrt(n)
Type I error (alpha) = rejecting true null (false positive); Type II error (beta) = accepting false null
Power = 1 - beta (probability of detecting a true effect)
Mean affected by outliers; Median best for skewed data; Mode = most frequent value

subject: Forensic Medicine
title: Important IPC / Legal Sections - Must-Know One-Liners
points: IPC 299/300 = Culpable homicide / Murder definitions
IPC 302 = Punishment for murder
IPC 304 = Culpable homicide not amounting to murder
IPC 304-A = Death by negligence (relevant to medical negligence)
IPC 304-B = Dowry death
IPC 320 = Grievous hurt (8 types)
IPC 376 = Punishment for rape
IPC 312-316 = Criminal abortion / causing miscarriage
IPC 84 = Insanity as a defense (McNaughten rule)
Section 174 CrPC = Police inquest; Section 176 CrPC = Magistrate inquest
Section 53/54 CrPC = Medical examination of accused
Section 45 IEA = Expert opinion (doctor as expert witness)
Section 32 IEA = Dying declaration (admissible)
MTP Act 1971 (amended 2021): up to 20 weeks (one doctor), 20-24 weeks special categories (two doctors)
PCPNDT Act 1994 = bans prenatal sex determination
Mental Healthcare Act 2017 = largely decriminalized attempt to suicide

subject: Anatomy
title: Cranial Nerve & Brachial Plexus Lesions
rows: Lower half of face only (forehead spared)
Loss of gag reflex, dysphagia, uvula deviation

subject: Pediatrics
title: Pediatric Milestones & Reflexes
rows: Crawls, pincer grasp begins, stranger anxiety
Stands alone, walks with support, 1-2 words, mature pincer
Climbs stairs, 2-word sentences, copies vertical line
Rides tricycle, copies circle, knows name & age

subject: Microbiology
title: Toxins & Their Mechanism to Organism
rows: V. cholerae - activates Gs, raises cAMP (watery diarrhea)
C. botulinum - blocks ACh release (flaccid paralysis)
B. pertussis - inhibits Gi, raises cAMP

subject: Surgery
title: Triads, Tetrads & Pentads - High Yield
rows: Charcot's triad + Hypotension + Altered mental status
Hypotension, Muffled heart sounds, Raised JVP (tamponade)
Hypoglycemia symptoms, Low glucose, Relief with glucose (insulinoma)
2% population, 2 ft from ileocecal, 2 inches long, 2 types ectopic tissue
VSD, Overriding aorta, Pulmonary stenosis, RV hypertrophy
Dysphagia, Iron deficiency anemia, Esophageal web
